zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Verdun於1303年8月9日出生在英國史洛普爾斯坦頓 拉西時，她的父親Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun24歲，母親Maud Mortimer18歲。她於1317年4月28日與John de Montagu在直布羅陀金也斯 查阿培爾結婚。她去世於1334年10月2日在英國斯塔福爾阿頓享年31歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾可洛登可洛登 艾比。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Verdun于1303年8月9日出生在英国史洛普爾斯坦頓 拉西时，她的父亲Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun24岁，母亲Maud Mortimer18岁。她于1317年4月28日与John de Montagu在直布罗陀金也斯 查阿培爾结婚。她去世于1334年10月2日在英国斯塔福爾阿頓享年31岁，埋葬在英国斯塔福爾可洛登可洛登 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Verdun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Virden
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Lucien, Marcel, Pierre, Renaud, Sylvie.
French: habitational name from (Le) Verdon, the name of several places in various parts of France, for example in Dordogne, Gironde, and Marne. Compare   Ferdon  .
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Verdun, the name of a fief in Vessey (Manche), France. Compare   Verdun  .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Verdun was born on 9 August 1303, in Stanton Lacy, Shropshire, England, her father, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, was 24 and her mother, Maud Mortimer, was 18. She married John de Montagu on 28 April 1317, in King's Chapel, Gibraltar. She died on 2 October 1334, in Alton, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 31, and was buried in Croxden Abbey, Croxden, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Verdun est née le 9 août 1303, à Stanton Lacy, Shropshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Maud Mortimer, avait 18 ans. Elle a épousé John de Montagu le 28 avril 1317, à King's Chapel, Gibraltar. Elle est décédée le 2 octobre 1334, à Alton, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 31 ans, et a été enterrée à Croxden Abbey, Croxden, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Verdun am 9. August 1303 in Stanton Lacy, Shropshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun 24 und ihre Mutter Maud Mortimer 18. Sie hat am 28. April 1317 in King's Chapel, Gibraltar John de Montagu geheiratet. Sie starb am 2. Oktober 1334 im Alter von 31 Jahren in Alton, Staffordshire, England und wurde in Croxden Abbey, Croxden, Staffordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Verdun è nata il il 9 agosto 1303 nel Stanton Lacy, Shropshire, England, sua padre, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Maud Mortimer, aveva 18 anni. Lei sposato John de Montagu il 28 aprile 1317, nel King's Chapel, Gibilterra. Morì il 2 ottobre 1334, nel Alton, Staffordshire, England, all'età di 31 anni, e fu sepolta a Croxden Abbey, Croxden, Staffordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Verdunが1303年8月9日にイギリスシュロプシュアイアスタントン ラシで生まれた時，父Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdunは24歳，母Maud Mortimerは18歳であった。1317年4月28日にジブラルタルキング'ス チャペルでJohn de Montaguと結婚した。彼女は1334年10月2日にイギリススタフォードシュアイアアルトンで31歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアクロクスデンクロクスデン アビーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Verdun(이)가 1303년 8월 9일 영국 슈럽실르 스탠턴 레이시에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Mortimer(은)는 18세였다. 그녀는 1317년 4월 28일 지브롤터 킨그'스 차플에서 John de Montagu(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1334년 10월 2일 영국 스태퍼드실르 알턴에서 31세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 크록스든 크록스든 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.9 August 1303–2 October 1334: 9 de agosto de 1303–2 de outubro de 1334
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Verdun nasceu em 9 de agosto de 1303, em Stanton Lacy, Shropshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Maud Mortimer, tinha 18 anos. Ela casou-se com John de Montagu em 28 de abril de 1317, em King's Chapel, Gibraltar. Ela faleceu em 2 de outubro de 1334, em Alton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 31 anos, e foi sepultada em Croxden Abbey, Croxden, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Verdun родилась 9 августа 1303 г. в местности стантон ласи, шропшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Maud Mortimer, 18. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John de Montagu 28 апреля 1317 г. в местности кинг'с чапел, Гибралтар. Она умерла 2 октября 1334 г. в местности алтон, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 31 год и была похоронена в местности кроксден эбби, кроксден, стаффордшир, Англия.

es.9 August 1303–2 October 1334: 9 de agosto de 1303–2 de octubre de 1334
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Verdun nació el 9 de agosto de 1303, en Stanton Lacy, Shropshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Theobald de Verdun 2nd Lord Verdun, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Maud Mortimer, tenía 18 años. Se casó con John de Montagu el 28 de abril de 1317, en King's Chapel, Gibraltar. Murió el 2 de octubre de 1334, en Alton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 31 años, y fue sepultada en Croxden Abbey, Croxden, Staffordshire, Inglaterra.
